# Error Handling Checklist: Codex marketplace installation path

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-004 requirement quality for bounded install, update, remove, stale-cache, generated-payload, restart, and deeper-troubleshooting handoff guidance.
**Created**: 2026-06-14
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist error-handling` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Stale Update Checkpoints

- [x] CHK001 Does DOC-004 distinguish marketplace source, generated payload, installed plugin cache, and custom-agent destination so users know which surface to inspect when an install behaves unexpectedly? [Spec FR-002, FR-005; Data Model §Codex Surface]
- [x] CHK002 Do the docs require a bounded stale-after-update checkpoint that tells users what to check when the plugin appears stale after update, without expanding into DOC-008 troubleshooting depth? [Resolved: Spec FR-019; Plan §Design & Contracts; Data Model §Stale Update Checkpoint]
- [x] CHK003 Do the docs require observable cache/source mismatch symptoms, such as stale skill copy, old plugin metadata, unchanged custom-agent TOML behavior, or copied personal payload drift, while keeping diagnosis shallow? [Resolved: Spec FR-019; Data Model §Stale Update Checkpoint; Quickstart §Manual Content Review]
- [x] CHK004 Does the spec warn that editing the installed plugin cache is not the supported fix and that users should refresh the marketplace source or generated payload instead? [Spec FR-005]

## Custom-Agent Update Recovery

- [x] CHK005 Does DOC-004 explain that plugin installation and custom-agent registration are separate steps? [Spec FR-009, FR-010]
- [x] CHK006 Does restart guidance cover plugin enablement changes, custom-agent installation, and config edits that affect plugin or skill state? [Spec FR-012]
- [x] CHK007 Do the docs specify when users should rerun `$install` after plugin updates that change bundled custom-agent TOML files, then restart Codex before expecting updated agents? [Resolved: Spec FR-012; Data Model §Custom-Agent Checklist; Contract §Required Route Sections]
- [x] CHK008 Does verification remain observational and avoid adding a new doctor/list command or manual cache/TOML editing procedure? [Spec Session 2; Data Model §Custom-Agent Checklist]

## Personal Marketplace And Generated Payload Errors

- [x] CHK009 Do personal/local marketplace examples point to a copied or synced generated Codex payload rather than the mixed `speckit-pro/` authoring source tree? [Spec FR-004; Research Decision 3]
- [x] CHK010 Does repo-scoped marketplace wording allow `source.path` to point at `./dist/codex/speckit-pro` from `.agents/plugins/marketplace.json` while keeping personal/local examples separate? [Spec Session 1; Data Model §Install Path]
- [x] CHK011 Does the checklist guard against stale copied payloads by requiring users to refresh the copied personal/local payload before expecting source changes to appear? [Spec Session 1; Data Model §State Transitions]

## Bounded Handoff To Deeper Troubleshooting

- [x] CHK012 Does DOC-004 preserve links or deferrals to DOC-007 and DOC-008 for reference, troubleshooting, security, update/remove, rollback, and stale-cache depth? [Spec FR-014; Contract §Forbidden Content]
- [x] CHK013 Do requirements specify clear next links from the bounded stale/update checkpoint to deeper DOC-008 troubleshooting and DOC-007 reference material? [Resolved: Spec FR-019; Contract §Required Route Sections; Quickstart §Manual Content Review]
- [x] CHK014 Do forbidden-content requirements prevent DOC-004 from becoming a full update/remove/rollback or stale-cache forensics guide? [Contract §Forbidden Content]

## Notes

- Initial pass found four error-handling gaps around bounded stale-after-update checkpoints, cache/source mismatch symptoms, rerunning `$install` after custom-agent updates, and explicit next-link requirements.
- Gap remediation stayed documentation/planning-only and did not change manifests, generated payloads, installer behavior, TOML templates, hooks, marketplace behavior, release automation, or plugin runtime code.

## Verification Pass

- [x] CHK015 Re-run confirms the spec includes a bounded stale-after-update checkpoint and measurable stale-behavior success criterion. [Spec FR-019; Spec SC-007]
- [x] CHK016 Re-run confirms rerun `$install` plus restart is required after bundled custom-agent TOML updates. [Spec FR-012; Contract §Required Route Sections]
- [x] CHK017 Re-run confirms cache/source mismatch symptoms are named without adding DOC-008 troubleshooting depth. [Data Model §Stale Update Checkpoint; Contract §Forbidden Content]
- [x] CHK018 Re-run confirms next links point to DOC-008 for troubleshooting/update/remove/rollback and DOC-007 for reference details. [Spec FR-019; Quickstart §Manual Content Review]
